principles: Discover supported metrics and capabilities dynamically before applying rules.
Use Azure Cost Management actual cost as the primary cost signal.
Use Azure Retail Prices API for target option comparison and what-if estimates.
Use static P-tier tables only as fallback or enrichment when Azure APIs do not expose tier limits directly.
Prefer the best valid production target, not just the next lower or next higher tier.

lowCallCollectionPlan.goal: Collect disk properties, metrics, cost, and price data with the fewest REST calls while preserving recommendation quality.
callReductionPrinciples: Use subscription or resource-group list APIs instead of one GET per disk.
Cache metric definitions by resource type, location, namespace, and API version.
Fetch multiple disk metric names in one Azure Monitor metrics request using comma-separated metricnames.
Use Cost Management grouped by ResourceId once per scope instead of querying cost per disk.
Use Retail Prices once per region and disk meter group, cache paged results, and reuse for all disks in that region.
Only fetch related VM, NIC, or activity-log details when disk metrics or properties indicate a recommendation needs attachment context.

minimumCallFlowPerSubscription.name: Fetch all required disk metrics
minimumCallFlowPerSubscription.callPattern: 1 metrics call per disk per lookback window using comma-separated metricnames

minimumCallFlowPerSubscription.name: Fetch actual cost once per billing scope

minimumCallFlowPerSubscription.name: Fetch prices by region and cache
minimumCallFlowPerSubscription.callPattern: 1 Retail Prices query per region and managed-disk meter group, with paging

minimumCallFlowPerSubscription.name: Drill into related resources only when needed

frequent attach recommendation needs activity history
security policy requires related DiskAccess or Key Vault context
snapshot or backup recommendation needs recovery context

inventoryOnly.recommendationDepth: unattached disks, basic SKU/size cost, encryption, network access, reservation candidates

calls: one metrics call per disk with all required metric names

calls: attached VM model and VM metrics
deepAssessment.recommendationDepth: VM bottleneck analysis, frequent attach, host caching, recovery posture

recommendedMetricRequest.singleDiskUrlTemplate: https://management.azure.com/{diskResourceId}/providers/Microsoft.Insights/metrics?api-version=2023-10-01&metricnamespace=Microsoft.Compute/disks&metricnames=Composite Disk Read Bytes/sec,Composite Disk Write Bytes/sec,Composite Disk Read Operations/sec,Composite Disk Write Operations/sec,DiskPaidBurstIOPS&aggregation=average,maximum,total&interval={interval}&timespan={start}/{end}

conditionalDrilldownRules.trigger: disk is attached and IOPS or throughput is near limit
extraCalls: get attached VM or use cached VM inventory
conditionalDrilldownRules.reason: VM cached and uncached storage limits may be the real bottleneck.

conditionalDrilldownRules.trigger: disk is unattached, reserved, idle, or expensive
extraCalls: backup or snapshot inventory if retention decision is required
conditionalDrilldownRules.reason: Deletion and snapshot recommendations require recovery validation.

conditionalDrilldownRules.trigger: bursting is enabled or DiskPaidBurstIOPS is nonzero
conditionalDrilldownRules.reason: Burst optimization can usually be assessed from disk metrics, cost, and price data.

lowCallCollectionPlan.analyzerRuntimeCalls: none; analyzer reads normalized DB records and this rule file

requiredProductionFilters: target option must satisfy p99 IOPS plus production buffer
target option must satisfy p99 throughput plus production buffer
target option must satisfy required disk capacity
target option must be available in the disk region
target option must be compatible with attached VM and attachment mode
target option must not weaken required security posture
target option must not weaken required availability posture
target option must respect workload constraints such as OS disk support, shared disk support, host caching support, and zone support

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'properties.diskState') eq 'Unattached' or get_value(resource, 'properties.managedBy') missing True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Delete unattached managed disk after retention policy, or snapshot then delete when recovery is required.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Compare actual monthly disk cost with snapshot cost estimate and retention policy.

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.idleDays') gte 30 and get_value(resource, 'signals.retentionRequired') eq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Create snapshot or backup, then delete the full provisioned disk.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Use snapshot when restore requirement exists; otherwise delete.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: disk is idle or unattached and retention is required

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'properties.sku.name') eq 'Premium_LRS' and get_value(resource, 'signals.p95IopsUtilizationPct') lt 40 and get_value(resource, 'signals.p95ThroughputUtilizationPct') lt 40

recommendationRules.recommendation: Move to the cheapest valid target that satisfies p99 IOPS, p99 throughput, disk size, production buffer, region availability, and VM compatibility.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Enumerate P-levels, Premium SSD v2, Standard SSD, and ZRS options. Filter by production requirements. Score by cost, headroom, reliability, security, and operational risk.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: sku.name == 'Premium_LRS' AND p95Iops < 40% of currentTier.baseIops AND p95ThroughputMBps < 40% of currentTier.baseThroughputMBps for 14-30 days

recommendationRules.recommendation: Upgrade P-tier, use expanded performance where applicable, or evaluate Premium SSD v2 or Ultra Disk for granular provisioned performance.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Select the lowest-cost target that satisfies p99 demand plus production buffer without violating VM limits.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: sku.name == 'Premium_LRS' AND (p95Iops > 80% of currentTier.baseIops OR p95ThroughputMBps > 80% of currentTier.baseThroughputMBps)

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.burstFrequencyPct') gt 0 or get_value(resource, 'properties.burstingEnabled') eq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Keep burst for rare spikes. If burst is frequent or sustained, compare paid burst cost with a higher tier, expanded performance, Premium SSD v2, or Ultra Disk.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: If burstFrequencyPct is low and cost is low, keep. If frequent, recommend cheapest stable performance target.

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'properties.sku.name') eq 'Premium_LRS' and get_value(resource, 'signals.steadyUsage') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.expectedLifetimeDays') gte 365

recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Recommend reservation only when actual cost, retail reservation price, workload stability, and expected lifetime justify commitment.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: sku.name == 'Premium_LRS' AND tier in ['P30','P40','P50','P60','P70','P80'] AND diskAgeDays > 30 AND usage is stable AND expectedLifetimeDays >= 365

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.productionCritical') eq True and get_value(resource, 'properties.sku.name') eq 'Premium_LRS' and get_value(resource, 'signals.regionSupportsPremiumZrs') eq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Evaluate Premium_ZRS for zone-resilient production workloads.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Prefer ZRS when reliability requirement outweighs price delta.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: productionCritical == true AND sku.name == 'Premium_LRS' AND region supports Premium_ZRS AND zoneFailureResilienceRequired == true

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.p95VmCachedIopsConsumedPct') gte 80 or get_value(resource, 'signals.p95VmUncachedIopsConsumedPct') gte 80 or get_value(resource, 'signals.p95VmCachedBandwidthConsumedPct') gte 80 or get_value(resource, 'signals.p95VmUncachedBandwidthConsumedPct') gte 80

recommendationRules.recommendation: Resize VM, redistribute disks, adjust host caching, or move workload to a VM size with higher storage limits.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Do not recommend disk upgrade if VM limit is the bottleneck unless VM resize is included.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: sumAttachedDiskP95Iops approaches VM cached or uncached IOPS limit OR sumAttachedDiskP95Throughput approaches VM throughput limit

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.readRatio') gte 0.8 and get_value(resource, 'signals.diskSupportsHostCaching') eq True

recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Recommend only after checking VM cache capability and workload consistency.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: readRatio > 0.8 AND disk supports host caching AND workload is read-heavy

recommendationRules.recommendation: Avoid ReadWrite host caching unless application consistency and durability requirements are explicitly validated.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Prefer None for write-heavy production data disks unless workload documentation supports ReadWrite.

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'properties.publicNetworkAccess') neq 'Disabled' or get_value(resource, 'signals.publicExportAllowed') eq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Restrict disk import/export with DiskAccess private endpoints and disable public network access where possible.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Preserve operational workflows while reducing public exposure.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: properties.publicNetworkAccess != 'Disabled' OR properties.networkAccessPolicy allows public export or upload

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'policy.requiresCustomerManagedKeys') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.usesCustomerManagedKey') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Use disk encryption sets with customer-managed keys, or platform plus customer keys if double encryption is required.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Recommend CMK only when required by policy, compliance, or tenant baseline.

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.sensitiveWorkload') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.securityProfileMeetsPolicy') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Use Trusted Launch or Confidential VM compatible disk security based on workload requirement.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Match security profile to policy without forcing unsupported migration paths.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: osDisk == true AND sensitiveWorkload == true AND requiredSecurityType is stronger than properties.securityProfile.securityType

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'properties.sku.name') eq 'Standard_LRS' and get_value(resource, 'properties.osType') present True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Migrate Standard HDD OS disks to Standard SSD or Premium SSD before retirement of Standard HDD OS disk support.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Choose Standard SSD for lower-cost production-compatible baseline or Premium SSD for performance-sensitive workloads.

selectionAlgorithm: Get current disk resource and attachment context.
Discover metric definitions for the disk resource.
Fetch all relevant supported metrics for a production lookback window.
Calculate p95 and p99 IOPS and throughput.
Fetch actual cost for the current disk from Cost Management.
Fetch retail prices for candidate target disk options in the same region.
Fetch VM storage limits and compatibility for attached VM.
Build candidate targets from dynamic SKU and price data, enriched by static fallback tables.
Remove candidates that fail production filters.
Score remaining candidates using configured weights.
Return the highest-scoring recommendation with reason, expected impact, risk, and required validation.

bestRecommendation.action: resize | migrate | reserve | delete | snapshot_then_delete | secure | no_action

knownLimitations: Microsoft.Compute/disks metrics do not currently expose filesystem used capacity.
Microsoft.Compute/disks metrics do not currently expose disk latency directly at the disk resource level.
Guest OS, VM Insights, application telemetry, or agent metrics are required for filesystem utilization, queue depth, and workload latency.
VM-level storage limits can cap disk performance even when disk tier has enough IOPS or throughput.
Static P-tier fallback data must be refreshed when Azure documentation or SKU behavior changes.

conditionLanguage.fieldPathRoot: normalized DB resource record passed to analyzer
conditionLanguage.unknownHandling: return investigate or cap classification; never mark stay/best from missing required data

classificationLevels.meaning: Already optimized or only minor optional improvements remain.
Generally healthy with low-risk optimization opportunities.
Needs review; measurable cost, performance, reliability, security, or governance risk exists.
Action recommended; resource is materially over-costed, underperforming, risky, or non-compliant.
Immediate action recommended; high waste, outage risk, security exposure, or compliance risk.

evaluationOrder: apply best conditions only when no warning or worst condition matched
apply scoring caps for missing data, security findings, reliability findings, and unknown ownership
return score, classification, matchedConditions, and deterministicRecommendations
notes: Do not parse recommendationRules.condition at runtime; those are descriptive only.
Python assessment must use pythonAssessment.deterministicCases plus normalized metrics/signals.
All runtime decisions must be reproducible from input JSON and this file.

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Current tier/size/configuration is acceptable; keep it and continue monitoring.
validWhen: required cost and metrics are present

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Move to a smaller/lower/cooler/cheaper SKU, tier, capacity, retention, or feature set while preserving production constraints.
validWhen: paid capacity or premium feature is unused
target capability satisfies observed demand and policy

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Move to a larger/higher/more resilient SKU, tier, capacity, throughput, or feature set because current configuration is constrained or risky.
validWhen: availability or security baseline requires a higher capability

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Stop, deallocate, archive, delete, expire, or remove the resource or child cost item after owner approval.
validWhen: resource is unattached, idle, expired, duplicate, orphaned, or non-production with no usage

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Do not resize automatically; collect missing data, resolve conflicting signals, or review owner/business context.

pythonAssessment.deterministicCasesSource: legacy scoring support; recommendation runtime should prefer assessmentRules/actionOutcomeRules evaluated against DB fields

runtimeContract.ruleSignalConvention: signals.rules.<recommendationRule.id> == true triggers that recommendation rule
collectorResponsibility: Collect Azure resource properties, Azure Monitor metrics, Cost Management rows, price cache, tags, policy, and advisor data outside recommendation runtime.
Store normalized values in the database using the roots resource, properties, metrics, cost, prices, tags, policy, advisor, and signals.
Calculate derived signals deterministically from stored DB values where raw metrics/properties need aggregation.
Do not require the analyzer to call Azure REST APIs while producing recommendations.
analyzerResponsibility: Load the resource assessment JSON by resourceType.
Load the normalized resource record from the database.
Evaluate rule condition groups with supported operators only.
Return matched outputs including recommendationAction/actionOutcome, message, severity, pillar, resultCode, and evidence.

costManagementCharging.retailPriceUse: Use Retail Prices API for target estimates only; use Cost Management for actual current cost.
primaryChargeDrivers: provisioned IOPS/throughput for Ultra/Premium SSD v2
reservation for eligible Premium SSD tiers

analyzerContract.purpose: FastAPI/backend analyzer loads this JSON as deterministic optimization rules and evaluates them against metrics/properties/cost already stored in the database.

normalizedInputRecord.resource: identity and inventory fields such as id, name, type, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, location, sku, kind, tags, and lifecycle timestamps
normalizedInputRecord.properties: latest ARM/Resource Graph properties stored by collector
normalizedInputRecord.metrics: Azure Monitor metric aggregates stored by collector; expected aggregate suffixes include Avg, Min, Max, P50, P90, P95, P99, Count, Total, TrendPct, and WindowDays where available
normalizedInputRecord.cost: Cost Management actual/amortized/forecast rows normalized by resourceId, meter, skuName, product, chargeType, pricingModel, reservation/savings-plan coverage, and period totals
normalizedInputRecord.prices: optional Retail Prices/API or internal price cache for candidate comparison
normalizedInputRecord.policy: environment, production flag, criticality, required baseline, business hours, retention, and compliance requirements
normalizedInputRecord.signals: derived booleans/numbers calculated by collector/analyzer from DB metrics, properties, cost, and policy

conditionEvaluation.numericComparisonBehavior: compare only when stored value is numeric; otherwise rule_not_applicable
conditionEvaluation.stringComparisonBehavior: case_insensitive for contains/in/not_in when analyzer normalizes strings
conditionEvaluation.arrayComparisonBehavior: contains checks scalar membership or substring membership depending on stored value type
conditionEvaluation.booleanComparisonBehavior: accept true/false booleans; analyzer may normalize string booleans before evaluation

evaluationOrder: load resource-specific assessment file by resourceType
read one normalized resource record from DB with resource/properties/metrics/cost/tags/policy/signals/prices/advisor roots
evaluate data completeness and missing-cost rules
evaluate stop_or_delete rules before resize rules
evaluate upgrade rules before downgrade when both saturation and low-utilization signals conflict
evaluate stay rules only when no higher-priority action matched
return matched rule outputs with recommendationAction/actionOutcome and evidence paths

outputContract.actionFieldMeaning: normalized machine action outcome, one of stay/downgrade/upgrade/stop_or_delete/investigate
